channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's funeral ceremonies begin in Iraq and Qom
item.description: The funeral processions for late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei have commenced, with his remains transported to Iraq for public ceremonies. Khamenei, who died in February during US-Israeli strike...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates with military strikes and oil sanctions amid Hormuz tensions
item.description: The United States has launched a series of military strikes against Iran, targeting key infrastructure and naval assets in retaliation for attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. This f...

item.title: US Conducts Strikes on Iran in Response to Hormuz Ship Attacks
item.description: The US military carried out significant strikes against Iran following attacks on three commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. These strikes, announced by the US Central Command, are a direct res...

item.title: Ram Temple Trust Controversy: Champat Rai Denies Approval of Cash-Counting Guidelines, Blames SBI
item.description: Champat Rai, former general secretary of the Ram Temple trust, has denied approving the 2025 cash-counting guidelines and accused the State Bank of India (SBI) of negligence. In a submission to the Sp...

item.title: South Africa Warns Against Coastline Contact Amid Avian Influenza Outbreak
item.description: Wildlife authorities in South Africa have issued urgent warnings to the public to avoid contact with coastal wildlife and refrain from touching or moving sick or dead seabirds, as a highly pathogenic ...

item.title: University of Victoria launches second satellite to boost space industry
item.description: The University of Victoria (UVic) on Vancouver Island has launched its second satellite, marking a significant step in its ambition to establish itself as a key player in Canada's growing space indust...

item.title: World Cup highlights: Messi's emotional reaction, Quaresma's awkward interview, and England's celebratory embrace controversy
item.description: The article compiles various moments from the FIFA World Cup, highlighting key events and reactions. Lionel Messi was seen in tears after Argentina's decisive 3-0 victory over Egypt, with the team sec...

item.title: City Reviews Knox Mountain Park Caretaker Role Amid Community Concerns
item.description: Concerns are growing among residents and park users about the future of the live-in caretaker position at Knox Mountain Park in Kelowna, B.C., as the city reviews the role following the retirement of ...

item.title: Western Australia's Secret Harbour by-election: One Nation's influence and Andrew Hastie's role
item.description: The Secret Harbour by-election in Western Australia has been triggered by the retirement of Paul Papalia, a senior state government minister, due to a family illness. This seat, created for the 2025 s...

item.title: CBI files first chargesheet against seven accused in Rs 4,097 crore RCFL bank fraud investigation
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has filed its first chargesheet in the alleged Rs 4,097 crore Reliance Commercial Finance Ltd (RCFL) bank fraud case before a special CBI court in Mumbai. The...

item.title: Pakistan to Implement AI-Driven Tax System for Efficient Revenue Management
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced at a Karachi banking summit that Pakistan is adopting an AI-led tax administration model to minimize human intervention between taxpayers and the governme...

item.title: Astronomers Discover Mysterious Compound on Pluto and Titan
item.description: Researchers using the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) have detected an unexplained chemical signature on both Pluto and Saturn’s moon Titan. The absorption band centered at 5.113 micrometers does no...

item.title: Durban to Negotiate Host City Agreement for 2027 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Durban is set to enter negotiations for a host city agreement with Cricket South Africa to stage the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup in October 2027. The eThekwini Municipality will submit a non-committal...

item.title: Philippines and Canada elevate ties to strategic partnership under Marcos administration
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced the elevation of bilateral relations to a strategic partnership during Marcos' official visit to Canada. This move mark...

item.title: Legal proceedings delayed due to prisoner's dietary rights dispute
item.description: Radovan Krejcir, a convicted Czech crime boss serving a 35-year sentence at Kgosi Mampuru II Correctional Centre, caused a delay in his court proceedings by refusing to testify. He alleged that prison...

item.title: Farage's Rivals Withdraw from Clacton By-Election Amid Financial Scrutiny
item.description: The article details how major UK political parties have collectively decided not to contest the upcoming Clacton by-election, triggered by Nigel Farage's resignation as Reform UK leader. Farage has an...

item.title: Wayanad Twin Tunnel Project Faces Scrutiny After Landslide Kills 3
item.description: A devastating landslide near the Wayanad twin tunnel project site in Kerala's Wayanad district killed three laborers and left five missing, sparking renewed scrutiny of the controversial infrastructur...

item.title: U2 unveil new single 'Street of Dreams' produced by Jacknife Lee
item.description: U2 have shared the first taster from their forthcoming album, the Jacknife Lee-produced 'Street Of Dreams'. The video for the single was filmed in Mexico City, where fans gathered around a bus as the ...

item.title: Rita Edochie denounces double standards in marriage advice over infidelity comments
item.description: Nollywood actress Rita Edochie has condemned a wedding MC for making controversial remarks about infidelity during a ceremony. The MC reportedly told a wife that cheating would lead to death and madne...

item.title: Osun journalists get training and grants to boost accountability reporting ahead of election
item.description: DevReporting, in partnership with the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID), has launched a training and story grant program for journalists in Osun State ahead of the 2026 governors...
